content:
* We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation. For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day. We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region. We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands. We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture. We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe. Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui. Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région. Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales. Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine. Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________
sentences: * We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation.
For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day.
We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region.
We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands.
We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture.
We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe.
Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui.
Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région.
Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales.
Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine.

Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________

content:
* Finished SmartServe training
* Participated in 101 Week as a guide and attended most events
* Ran the Engineering Jeopardy event with Ashna which was very successful
* Assisted 101ers by answering various questions
* Coordinated the clubs fair tabling and helped print posters for uOEC
* Oversaw Messy Day cleanup
* Sorted lots of trash/recycling
* Worked on cleaning the office at various times throughout 101 Week and did a major cleanup this week
* Put into storage the equipment from Messy Day, stuff that was in A04, and from the office
* Discussed new TV in A04 with facilities manager and faculty IT, it will display ESS ads
* Sent email to request details about painting the office, drafted a budget amendment motion for BoD
* Sent email inquiring about installing charging ports in SITE lecture halls
* Refactored the website to upgrade it from Gatsby v2 to v5 and host it with Cloudflare Workers
* Answering questions and meeting with groups interested in becoming Sub-Associations
* Sent follow-up email to the UOSU/SÉUO regarding RSG/GÉR recognition of representative groups
* Requested VEMS credentials for ChESS/AÉGC, CESA/AÉGCI, and IEEE
* Was notified that Sub-Associations have had their club status revoked
* I’m confused and waiting for info from the UOSU/SÉUO
* Spoke with the UOSU’s Sustainable Development Centre’s coordinator regarding sustainability and 101 week, looking to find a solution for disposing of the old t-shirts
* Attended the uOEC/CIuO committee meeting and overseeing the committee's work
* Spoke to the faculty’s secretary regarding STM 117 availability
* Created a uOEC/CIuO Discord server (copied from last year’s)
* Attended part of the IEEE meeting where elections were conducted and raised concerns with potential Sub-Association Policy non-compliance
* Drafted an email regarding EEF/FdG governance, waiting for the Co-presidents to review it
* Met with Andrew Sowinski (Vice-Dean, Programs) to discuss plans for accreditation of experiential learning (JMTS/ECJM, uOEC/CIuO, and other projects)
* Submitted an EEF/FdG application for OEC/CIO
* Reached out to the Ontario Ambassador of the CFES/FCÉG to ask about multidisciplinary design students’ eligibility to attend CEC/CCI
* Coordinated and designed the office hours schedule
* Office code was changed (the new code is top secret 🤫), a key will be ready soon
* Wrote a motion to call a special general meeting (didn’t reach quorum unfortunately)
* Created Community Engagement Navigator posting #259 for recording 101 week volunteer hours

content:
* We have a lot of open positions so please advertise it and get people to run
* Fall one-on-ones
* I want to meet with all the execs and get a feeling for the next semester and how mandates are going!
* Interims; we will talk after the by-elections
* Managers I would like a meeting with you guys but it’s not as needed
* Commissioners, unless you really want to talk to me (i mean who wouldn’t) I don’t need a meeting
* Accountability
* I realised mid way through the summer that people have more going on then just school so I was a little bit more relaxed on accountability but not anymore :)
* Updates
* Officer meeting: Before the meeting
* BoD: Thursday at 11am
* Attendance
* Officer meeting & BoD: Mandatory. Absences need to be communicated in advance to either me or the co-presidents
* Office hours
* IF YOU ARE ON THE SCHEDULE SHOW UP plz
* If you can’t attend them, try to get them covered! The conversation doesn’t need to be in the #office-hours chat but the conclusion does.
* I.e. If someone is covering your office let me know! Again in advance preferably
* I will be a basement dweller so I will be keeping an eye out
